name: Nutella Ice Cream Torte
recipeIngredient: 2 pints nut or chocolate chip ice cream or frozen yogurt, softened
2 pints strawberry ice cream or frozen yogurt, softened
2 pints vanilla ice cream or frozen yogurt, softened
recipeInstructions:
1. Spoon 3 tablespoons Nutella into a small pastry bag with a small plain tip. Set aside to use for topping.
2. Wrap the outside of a 9-inch-diameter springform pan with foil that has 2 3/4-inch-high sides.
3. Spread nut or chocolate chip ice cream evenly in the bottom of the pan.
4. Using a small spoon, drop half of the remaining Nutella by teaspoonfuls over the ice cream, spacing drops evenly (do not spread). Freeze until firm, about 30 minutes.
5. Spread strawberry ice cream evenly over the frozen mixture.
6. Drop the remaining Nutella by teaspoonfuls over the strawberry ice cream, spacing drops evenly (do not spread). Freeze until firm, about 30 minutes.
7. Spread vanilla ice cream evenly over the frozen mixture.
8. Using a spatula, smooth the top.
9. Pipe the reserved Nutella decoratively over the top and freeze just until the Nutella is firm, about 30 minutes.
10. Wrap the torte in plastic and freeze at least 6 hours or overnight (can be prepared up to 1 week ahead). Keep frozen.
11. Let the torte stand briefly at room temperature to soften slightly. Remove foil and plastic from the pan.
12. Using a knife, cut around the sides of the pan and release the sides.
13. Press hazelnuts into the sides of the torte, if desired.
This recipe consists of three flavors of ice cream or frozen yogurt layered with Nutella, a creamy hazelnut-chocolate spread available in specialty foods stores and most supermarkets (look for it where the peanut butter is sold). The Nutella freezes firmly on the ice cream but is soft enough to cut through.
